skill.name: invoice-ocr-sorter
skill.description: Organizes invoice and bill screenshots or PDFs into categorized spreadsheet-ready records using AI-assisted OCR.
skill.summary: Turn invoice and bill images into organized expense tables for review.
skill.author: Hermes-Sedimentary

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptography at SKILL.md:4 and high entropy at SKILL.md:1. Manual review found only front matter, Chinese descriptive text, and usage notes, with no executable code, obfuscation, network access, or prompt injection. The alerts are false positives, so the skill is suitable for publication with low residual risk.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The flagged line is the skill description in front matter. It describes invoice and bill OCR sorting and does not contain cryptographic code, algorithm selection, or credential handling.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirms line 4 is descriptive metadata only. No cryptographic API, weak algorithm, or executable instruction is present.

low_findings.title: Static Finding Dismissed: High File Entropy
low_findings.description: The flagged entropy signal appears to come from multilingual metadata and symbols in a short Markdown file. The file is readable text and contains no binary, encrypted, or encoded payload.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The full scanned file is plain Markdown with YAML front matter. No obfuscated content or hidden executable material was found.

content.value_statement: Manual invoice sorting wastes time and creates inconsistent expense records. This skill guides AI OCR extraction, categorization, and spreadsheet-ready organization for invoices and bills.
actual_capabilities: Guides OCR extraction from invoice or bill screenshots and PDFs.
Helps classify extracted invoice data into expense categories.
Structures results for Excel-style tables and manual review.
Supports workflows for personal users, freelancers, small businesses, and teams.
Reminds users to verify important AI-generated financial outputs.
limitations: Does not include a dedicated OCR engine or executable processing script.
Accuracy depends on image quality, document layout, and model vision support.
Financial records should be reviewed before tax or accounting use.
May need user-provided category rules for consistent bookkeeping.

use_cases.title: Prepare Monthly Expense Sheets
use_cases.description: Convert uploaded bills into a clean table grouped by vendor, date, amount, and category.

use_cases.title: Organize Client Reimbursements
use_cases.description: Extract invoice details from project purchases and prepare a reviewable reimbursement summary.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Bill Intake
use_cases.description: Create a shared process for turning mixed invoice files into consistent spreadsheet rows.

prompt_templates.title: Sort Basic Invoices
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner workflow for a small batch of files.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use invoice-ocr-sorter to extract vendor, date, amount, tax, and category from these invoices. Present the result as an Excel-ready table.

prompt_templates.title: Review Bills by Category
prompt_templates.scenario: Group recurring bills for monthly review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these bill images and group them by category. Flag unclear fields and show totals for each category.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Reimbursement Summary
prompt_templates.scenario: Prepare expense records for client or employer approval.
prompt_templates.prompt: Extract invoice details from these receipts and bills. Create a reimbursement summary with notes for missing or uncertain information.

prompt_templates.title: Apply Custom Bookkeeping Rules
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with user-defined categories and validation rules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use my category rules to classify each invoice. Normalize vendor names, identify duplicates, and list records that need manual verification.

output_examples.input: A PDF with three vendor invoices for software, shipping, and office supplies.
output: Spreadsheet-ready rows with vendor, invoice date, total, tax, and category.
A category subtotal section for software, shipping, and office supplies.
A short review list for any unreadable invoice numbers.

output_examples.input: Phone screenshots of restaurant and travel receipts from a business trip.
output: A reimbursement summary grouped by meals, transport, and lodging.
Detected dates and amounts with unclear fields marked for review.
A total claim amount suitable for manual approval.

best_practices: Upload clear images or PDFs with visible totals, dates, and vendor names.
Provide category rules before processing documents for consistent results.
Review extracted amounts and tax fields before using the table for accounting.
anti_patterns: Do not rely on OCR output as final tax or audit evidence without review.
Do not mix personal and business invoices without clear category guidance.
Do not upload sensitive financial documents to tools you are not authorized to use.

faq.question: Can this skill process both screenshots and PDFs?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill description targets screenshots and PDFs for invoice or bill OCR workflows.

faq.question: Does it create Excel files directly?
faq.answer: It guides the assistant to produce spreadsheet-ready tables. A separate export step may be needed.

faq.question: Can it categorize expenses automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, it can help classify invoices into categories. Clear user rules improve consistency.

faq.question: Is the output ready for accounting submission?
faq.answer: No. Review key fields such as totals, taxes, dates, and vendor names before submission.

faq.question: What users is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for individuals, freelancers, small businesses, and teams managing invoice records.

faq.question: Does the skill contain executable code?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed package contains a Markdown skill description and usage guidance only.
